title: Trusted-maintainer update relabels edge 'trusted' but leaves stale automated markers

findingDetails.evidence: The existingEdge update block sets only weight, context, and source_kind='trusted' (lines 92-94). It never deletes auto_added_at / auto_added_reason that an earlier automated write added at lines 108-109. Upgrading a previously-automated edge yields {source_kind:'trusted', auto_added_reason:'family-inference:...'} — contradictory provenance.
findingDetails.why: Provenance metadata becomes self-inconsistent: an edge marked trusted still carries an automated-origin reason/timestamp, defeating the purpose of the source_kind audit trail. The function is exported and the changelog advertises trusted-maintainer writes as a real capability, so this is a latent contract defect (currently exercised only by tests / direct API; no production caller passes 'trusted-maintainer').
findingDetails.recommendation: In the trusted update branch, delete existingEdge.auto_added_at and existingEdge.auto_added_reason when stamping source_kind='trusted' (and conversely never carry them on trusted writes), so provenance fields stay mutually consistent.
